Vendored third-party asset — Jedison
====================================

Package name : jedison
Version      : 1.13.0  (pinned exact in package.json devDependencies)
License      : MIT  (see ./LICENSE — Copyright (c) 2025 German Bisurgi)
Upstream repo: https://github.com/germanbisurgi/jedison
Docs         : https://germanbisurgi.github.io/jedison-docs/

Files copied (verbatim, unmodified) from the installed npm package:
  jedison.js      <- node_modules/jedison/dist/esm/jedison.js      (ESM build, default export)
  jedison.js.map  <- node_modules/jedison/dist/esm/jedison.js.map  (source map, dev only)
  LICENSE         <- node_modules/jedison/LICENSE

Why vendored
------------
The portal SPA (src/Portal/Public) is a no-build, browser ES-module app served
statically by @fastify/static under a strict CSP with NO CDN access. Jedison is
therefore copied here and imported by URL:

  import Jedison from '/vendor/jedison/jedison.js'

jedison is declared as a devDependency (not a runtime import from node_modules):
it is shipped as this static asset, so it must not appear in the production
dependency/audit graph.

Self-containment / offline notes
--------------------------------
- jedison has ZERO runtime npm dependencies; this single ESM file is the entire
  import graph (no relative sub-path imports — verified: the only module syntax
  in the file is the trailing `export { index as default }`).
- All third-party integrations (DOMPurify, Sortable, IMask, Choices, flatpickr,
  ace, Quill, etc.) are OPTIONAL and feature-detected via `window.<Global> && ...`;
  none are required by the default theme (`new Jedison.Theme()`), which renders
  plain HTML inputs/selects with `jedi-`-prefixed CSS classes.
- CSP-safe: the bundle contains NO `eval`, `new Function`, or inline event-handler
  attributes. DOM is built with createElement/addEventListener. (`innerHTML` is
  used only for trusted schema-supplied title/description text, optionally
  sanitized when window.DOMPurify is present.)

Do NOT edit these vendored files. To update, re-run:
  npm install jedison@<version> --save-dev --save-exact
and re-copy dist/esm/jedison.js + .map + LICENSE, then bump the version above.
